title: Ouratea vaccinioides
intro: Ouratea vaccinioides, also known as Vaccinoida or Ouratea, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Ochnaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ouratea vaccinioides include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Ouratea is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture in traditional herbal medicine, and is also used in the form of capsules or extracts in modern herbal remedies.
taxonomy: Ouratea vaccinioides is classified as a species of plant in the family Ochnaceae. This family is part of the order Malpighiales within the clade rosids. The order Malpighiales is in turn part of the clade eurosids I. The clade eurosids I is part of the larger clade rosidae. The clade rosidae is within the larger clade rosids.
